question.text: A GOI 10-year bond futures is priced at Rs. 104.50 and the underlying spot bond price is Rs. 104.20. If the cost of carry is 0.50%, what is the theoretical futures price?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Theoretical Futures Price = Spot × (1 + Cost of Carry) = 104.20 × (1 + 0.005) = 104.20 × 1.005 = Rs. 104.721 ≈ Rs. 104.72.
**The Trap:** Rs. 104.50 is the actual market price (which differs from theoretical). Rs. 104.70 simply adds 0.50 to spot. Rs. 105.20 adds 1.00. The cost of carry must be applied as a percentage of the spot price.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Theoretical futures pricing follows the cost-of-carry model across all asset classes. When actual futures differ from theoretical price, arbitrage opportunities exist (tested in NISM Series VIII).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The actual futures price (Rs. 104.50) being below theoretical (Rs. 104.72) suggests the futures are slightly underpriced — a potential reverse cash-and-carry arbitrage opportunity.
